title: Mount inside container fuse fs, is supported?
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I am trying to mount a fuse filesystem inside podman, but I got permision denied error:

```
$ podman run -it -v /cvmfs/alice.cern.ch:/cvmfs/alice.cern.ch ubuntu ls -lna /cvmfs
Error: container_linux.go:349: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:449: container init caused \"rootfs_linux.go:58: mounting \\\"/cvmfs/alice.cern.ch\\\" to rootfs \\\"/home/smosciat/.local/share/containers/storage/vfs/dir/9d748b830a1ac45f3d42b341ab47fe92851f7e16545aa1bf00d2ea9e24fe549a\\\" at \\\"/home/smosciat/.local/share/containers/storage/vfs/dir/9d748b830a1ac45f3d42b341ab47fe92851f7e16545aa1bf00d2ea9e24fe549a/cvmfs/alice.cern.ch\\\" caused \\\"operation not permitted\\\"\"": OCI runtime permission denied error
```
Is this behavior expected?

`podman` does work just fine if I try to mount inside the container the mounting directory of the host.

```
$ podman run -it -v /cvmfs/:/cvmfs/ ubuntu ls -lna /cvmfs
total 13
drwxr-xr-x  4 65534 65534    0 Apr 20 09:42 .
drwxr-xr-x 22     0     0 4096 Apr 20 09:50 ..
drwxr-xr-x 17 65534 65534 4096 Nov 27  2012 alice.cern.ch
drwxr-xr-x  3 65534 65534 4096 May 28  2014 cvmfs-config.cern.ch

```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

The step to reproduce are a little difficult, I will talk about `cvmfs`, but I guess it should apply to all FUSE filesystem

1. Install CVMFS and mount some repository in `/cvmfs/$repo_name`

2. Try to mount the repository inside the container

**Describe the results you received:**

Permission denied error

**Describe the results you expected:**

The filesystem mount

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Again, I am not sure this is not a know behavior. I just want to point it out to you guys and make sure it is know.

Also the `mount output`

```
$ mount | grep cvmfs
/etc/auto.cvmfs on /cvmfs type autofs (rw,relatime,fd=6,pgrp=1618,timeout=300,minproto=5,maxproto=5,indirect,pipe_ino=40535)
cvmfs2 on /cvmfs/cvmfs-config.cern.ch type fuse (ro,nosuid,nodev,relatime,user_id=0,group_id=0,default_permissions,allow_other)
cvmfs2 on /cvmfs/alice.cern.ch type fuse (ro,nosuid,nodev,relatime,user_id=0,group_id=0,default_permissions,allow_other)
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.9.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.1
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  gitCommit: ""
  goVersion: go1.10.1
  podmanVersion: 1.9.0
host:

  cpus: 4
  distribution:

    version: "18.04"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: student-ThinkPad
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1001
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 165536
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1001
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 165536
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.3.0-46-generic
  memFree: 192364544
  memTotal: 8084377600
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: 'containerd.io: /usr/bin/runc'
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc10
      commit: dc9208a3303feef5b3839f4323d9beb36df0a9dd
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns

      slirp4netns version 0.4.3
      commit: unknown
  swapFree: 1836576768
  swapTotal: 2147479552
  uptime: 3h 1m 22.21s (Approximately 0.12 days)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman/unknown,now 1.9.0~2 amd64 [installed]
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

```
$ cat /etc/os-release
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="18.04.4 LTS (Bionic Beaver)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian

```

If you guys needs more info I will be happy to help.

Also, feel free to just close this one if it is a know issues. I wasn't able to find it on github.

comments.body:
You are trying to remount the file system as writeable, while it is mounted (by root) as read only.

Could you try with `-v /cvmfs/alice.cern.ch:/cvmfs/alice.cern.ch:ro`?  (There is an additional `:ro`)

That worked perfectly! Thanks!

I didn't use the `:ro` because it wasn't necessary in Docker. Anyway we can close for me!
